nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated, found over rocky and sandy bottoms at depths of 0–23 m

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on coral polyps, crustaceans, and algae in the wild. In captivity has been reported to accept frozen and freeze-dried diets containing algae, supplemented with dried algae, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, and mysis shrimp. Feeding 2–3 times daily is generally advised.

imageCandidates.notes: Photo by Dennis White (1024x818). Sharp, well-lit live specimen against black background clearly showing the diagnostic orange dorsal coloration, white body with black spots, black eye band, and yellow tail.

summary: The Declivis Butterflyfish (Chaetodon declivis), also called the Marquesan Butterflyfish, is a rare Central Pacific species from the Marquesas and Line Islands. It displays striking orange dorsal coloration and a white body with black spots. Generally considered intermediate in difficulty and not reef-safe.
careNotes:
The Declivis Butterflyfish is generally considered a peaceful species and tends to do best in a well-established FOWLR aquarium with non-aggressive tankmates. It has been described as quite shy, so providing ample hiding places via live rock, caves, and crevices is generally advised.

This species is not considered reef-safe — it has been documented picking at coral polyps, feather dusters, anemones, and other small invertebrates. It is best kept in fish-only or FOWLR systems away from ornamental invertebrates.

Note on subspecies: FishBase recognizes two subspecies — the nominate *Chaetodon declivis declivis* from the Marquesas Islands (French Polynesia) and *C. d. wilderi* from Kiritimati (Christmas Island) in the Line Islands (Kiribati). The two subspecies may warrant elevation to full species status pending further research; care requirements are understood to be the same across both populations.

This species tends to form monogamous pair bonds, consistent with other butterflyfishes in the genus Chaetodon. Acquiring a mated pair when available is generally advised; established pairs are best kept together.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding has not been documented for this species under hobby conditions.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (FishBase: 12.0 cm TL vs LiveAquaria: 15.2 cm / 6 in), taxonomy, native range, and subspecies information. Size averaged to 10–14 cm range. FishBase does not provide aquarium husbandry parameters; water parameters sourced from LiveAquaria only. Reef2Reef wiki returned 403; BlueZoo Aquatics had no page for this species.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous; reported to form pairs during breeding in the wild. Captive breeding has not been documented under hobby conditions.
